body.title: [sprint] Inference router increment 5 — reconcile difficulty-bump escalation with device.py's existing loop
body.intention: I intend that a mechanical inference failure bumps the call's difficulty one bucket and re-routes through the SAME selector, so capability-escalation is one mechanism reusing the existing dispatch loop — not a parallel fork.
body.description:
**Problem:** the cost-optimizing selector (T-router-selector) is STATELESS — it picks the cheapest capable source for a given difficulty, but does not retry on failure. The epic's 'on mechanical failure, bump difficulty one bucket and re-run the same selector' is a DISPATCH-loop concern, and device.py ALREADY owns an escalation mechanism (escalation_hop, tier cascade — see the SYSTEM_ALARM 'no-provider' path and dispatch's escalation_hop param). Building a second bump loop inside route() would be a parallel fork of the thing that exists. Split out of T-router-selector on advisor guidance (2026-06-30) precisely because reconciling requires READING device.py's escalation first — its own unit of work.
**Proposed shape:** (1) READ device.py dispatch's current escalation (escalation_hop increment, tier cascade, how a hop re-selects). (2) Map the epic's 'bump difficulty one bucket' onto that: a MECHANICAL failure (exception/timeout/did-not-reach-DONE — NOT a quality judge, deferred) raises the required difficulty bucket by one rung (classify->code->design) and re-invokes the selector with the higher requirement. (3) TERMINATE cleanly: bumping past the top bucket (design) hard-fails with a terminal routing error + system_alarm, never loops. (4) Reconcile with / replace the existing escalation_hop tier cascade so there is ONE escalation mechanism, not two.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/device.py (dispatch escalation loop), rules_engine.py (route() gains an explicit difficulty/required-bucket override param so the loop can request a harder bucket), possibly dicksimnel/device.py (_TIER_CASCADE reconciliation).
**Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; NO SQLite; log every escalation hop (state change) at the crossing.
**Scope boundary:** the failure->bump->re-run loop + termination + reconciliation with existing escalation ONLY. The selector itself (T-router-selector) and Hex registration (T-ds-local-ollama-route) are done separately. Quality-based escalation (a quality judge) is explicitly OUT.
**Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) a mechanical failure on a code-bucket pick re-routes at the design bucket; (2) bump past design -> clean terminal failure + system_alarm, NO loop; (3) a successful first pick does NOT escalate; (4) only ONE escalation mechanism remains (no parallel _TIER_CASCADE + hop double-counting).
**Completion criteria:** a mechanical failure bumps required difficulty one bucket and re-runs the selector; termination past the top bucket is clean; device.py has a single escalation mechanism reconciled with the selector; tests green.

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
